questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The space race began in the mid-20th century. Q: The Soviet Union launched Sputnik, the first satellite. R: The US responded with the Apollo program. S: This competition led to significant technological advances.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: digital / has / way / the / changed / communication / we
options: Communication digital has changed the way we
We communicate way the has changed digital technology
Digital technology has changed the way we communicate
The way we communicate has changed digital technology
questions.solution: Digital technology has changed the way we communicate

questions.stem: Choose the synonym of ABUNDANT:

questions.stem: She is ____ for her contribution to science.

questions.stem: Synonym of LACONIC:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹20000, price = ₹100/unit, variable cost = ₹50/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Modified Accelerated Cost Recovery System (MACRS):
options: Tax depreciation system with accelerated write-offs
questions.solution: MACRS: IRS tax depreciation. Classes (3,5,7,10,15,20 year). Half-year convention. Accelerates deductions for tax benefits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the control chart shown (CL = 50, UCL = 59, LCL = 41), the highlighted point lying above the UCL indicates that the process is:
options: In control with only chance variation
Out of control (an assignable cause is present)
questions.solution: A point beyond the ±3σ control limits (UCL = 59) signals an assignable cause — the process is out of statistical control.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FAST diagram — critical path:
options: How? (left to right) and Why? (right to left). Basic function linked. Higher-order (left) vs lower-order (right)
questions.solution: FAST: Function Analysis System Technique. Bytheway method. Technical vs aesthetic functions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metaheuristics — simulated annealing:
options: Initialize T_high → random neighbor. Accept if better or with probability exp(-Δ/T). Annealing schedule
questions.solution: SA: Metropolis criterion. Cooling: T = α·T (α=0.95-0.99). Reheat if stuck. Traveling salesman, scheduling.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Simulation vs math modeling:
options: Handles complex systems without closed-form solutions

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Simulation warm-up period:
questions.solution: Warm-up: remove initial bias. System starts empty/biased. Let reach steady state before collecting statistics.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 6 min at a performance rating of 110%. The normal time is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Dual simplex method: maintains dual feasibility while seeking primal feasibility:
options: Maintains dual feasibility while seeking primal feasibility
Maintains primal feasibility seeking dual feasibility
questions.solution: Dual simplex: starts with dual feasible (primal may be infeasible). Works toward primal feasibility. Complementary to primal simplex.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: (Q,R) policy — continuous review:
options: Order Q when inventory hits R. R = μ_L + z·σ_L. Optimization: min K·D/Q + h(Q/2 + R - μ_L)
questions.solution: (Q,R): Q from EOQ. R from service level. Fill rate vs cycle service level. Approximate: Hadley-Whitin.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE — FAST diagramming:
options: How-Why logic. Basic function at top. Critical path through functions
questions.solution: FAST: left to right HOW? Right to left WHY? Scope lines define project boundaries.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Material handling principle: unit load means:
options: Moving items together in one load
questions.solution: Unit load: combine items into single move. Reduces trips, improves efficiency. Palletization, containerization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Capacity Requirements Planning (CRP) vs Rough-Cut Capacity Planning (RCCP):
options: CRP: detailed (work center level). RCCP: aggregate (key resources only)
questions.solution: RCCP: early check of MPS feasibility. CRP: detailed check after MRP run. Work center loading.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) for exponential distribution equals:
questions.solution: MTBF = 1/λ for constant failure rate. Memoryless property. MTBF is mean, not median. Half-life = ln(2)/λ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operating Characteristic (OC) curve:
options: Probability of acceptance vs lot fraction defective. n and c determine shape
questions.solution: OC: AQL (α-risk), LTPD (β-risk). α = 1 - P_a at AQL. β = P_a at LTPD.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LIFE cycle cost — net present value:
options: NPV = Σ(C_t/(1+r)^t). Initial + operating + maintenance + disposal. VE focused on operating phase (60% of LCC)
questions.solution: LCC: 20-30 year horizon for buildings. 30% initial, 60% O&M, 10% disposal. Discount rate ~10%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Traveling salesman problem (TSP):
options: Find minimum tour visiting n cities once. Held-Karp DP O(n²·2ⁿ). LKH (Lin-Kernighan) heuristic for large n
questions.solution: TSP: Euclidean, metric, asymmetric. 2-opt, 3-opt local search. Concorde (TSP solver) uses B&C.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has total task time 40 min, 5 stations and a cycle time of 10 min. Its balance efficiency is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Decision Tree Analysis: Expected Monetary Value at chance nodes:
questions.solution: EMV: Σ(Pi × Vi). Decision nodes: choose branch with highest EMV. Incorporates uncertainty into decisions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement comparing layouts is correct?
options: Product layout suits high-volume standardized output; process layout suits high variety
Process layout suits mass production best
questions.solution: Line/product layout = high volume, low variety; functional/process layout = low volume, high variety.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Taguchi Loss Function assumes:
options: Any deviation from target causes loss
questions.solution: Taguchi: L = k(y-T)². Loss increases quadratically from target. Contrast with goal-post mentality (pass/fail).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Freight — full truckload (FTL) vs LTL:
options: FTL: dedicated, ≤ capacity. LTL: shared, multiple stops. Less-than-truckload consolidates
questions.solution: FTL: lower cost/unit for full loads. LTL: cost = base + stop charges. Freight class (NMFC).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Theory of Constraints: drum-buffer-rope:
options: Schedule bottleneck (drum), protect it (buffer), control release (rope)
questions.solution: DBR: drum = bottleneck pace, buffer = protection, rope = controls input to match drum. Synchronous manufacturing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Capital equipment replacement — MAPI:
options: Machinery and Allied Products Institute. Urgency rating. Minimum cost life
questions.solution: MAPI: challenger vs defender. Adverse minimum: equivalent annual cost minimum. Tax implications.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Simple exponential smoothing forecast:
options: alpha times Actual(t) plus (1-alpha) times Forecast(t)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For D = 6400/yr, S = ₹50, H = ₹4/unit/yr, the minimum total ordering-plus-holding cost (at EOQ ≈ 400) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components of reliability 0.9 and 0.85 are in parallel. The system reliability is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Assembly-line balancing aims to:
options: Equalize workload across stations to minimize idle time
questions.solution: Line balancing assigns tasks so station times are nearly equal, reducing bottlenecks and idle time.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Manufacturing lead time includes:
options: Queue, setup, run, wait, and move times
questions.solution: Manufacturing LT: total order release to completion time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost ₹30000, price ₹120, variable cost ₹70/unit. The output needed for a target profit of ₹20000 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ABC analysis: A items are:
options: 10-20% of items, 70-80% of annual consumption value
questions.solution: Pareto: A items (high value) get tight control. B items (moderate). C items (many, low value).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Therbligs classification: effective vs ineffective:
options: Effective: reach, grasp, move, assemble. Ineffective: search, select, hold, delay
questions.solution: Effective: contribute to task. Ineffective: waste. Goal: minimize ineffective therbligs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity's normal time/cost are 10 days/₹3000 and crash time/cost are 4 days/₹5000. Its crash cost slope is:
questions.solution: Cost slope = (crash − normal cost)/(normal − crash time) = (5000−3000)/(10−4) = ₹333.3/day.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Causal — multiple regression:
options: Y = β₀ + β₁X₁ + β₂X₂ + ... + βₖXₖ + ε. Adjusted R², F-test, t-tests, multicollinearity
questions.solution: Multiple: VIF (variance inflation factor) > 10 → multicollinearity. Stepwise, forward, backward selection.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Regression forecasting: y = a + bx. Slope b =
questions.solution: Least squares: b = Σ(x-x̄)(y-ȳ)/Σ(x-x̄)². a = ȳ - bx̄. R² = explained variance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.5, last actual = 300 and last forecast = 180, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Weighted moving average:
options: Different weights to different periods (more weight to recent)
questions.solution: WMA: weights sum to 1. Recent period higher weight. Subjective weights.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Winters' method (triple exponential smoothing):
options: Level + trend + seasonality. Additive or multiplicative seasonality

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: EAC — Estimate at Completion:
options: EAC = BAC/CPI (typical). EAC = AC + (BAC-EV) (atypical). EAC = AC + (BAC-EV)/(CPI×SPI) (critical)
questions.solution: EAC: re-estimate. VAC = BAC - EAC. TCPI: to-complete performance index. CPI needed to hit budget.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Proportional hazards model (Cox):
options: h(t,Z) = h₀(t) · exp(b₁Z₁ + ... + bₙZₙ). Covariates: load, temperature, operator. Semi-parametric
questions.solution: Cox: baseline hazard h₀(t) arbitrary. β coefficients estimated by partial likelihood. HR = exp(β).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the break-even chart shown (fixed cost ₹10000, price ₹80, variable cost ₹60/unit), the marked point where revenue meets total cost occurs at:
questions.solution: BEP = FC/(p − v) = 10000/(80 − 60) = 500 units, where total revenue = total cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Total Quality Management (TQM) emphasizes:
options: Quality as the QC department's sole job
questions.solution: TQM is an organization-wide, customer-focused, continuous-improvement culture.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability bathtub — wear-out:
options: Increasing failure rate. Age-related: fatigue, corrosion, friction, wear. PM needed
questions.solution: Wear-out: PM interval before wear accelerates. Condition monitoring. Replacement before failure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a product (line) layout, machines are arranged:
options: In the sequence of operations for the product
questions.solution: Product layout lines up resources in process order for high-volume, standardized output.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Autocorrelation in forecast errors:
options: Correlation of errors with lagged versions. Indicates pattern not captured. Durbin-Watson test
questions.solution: Durbin-Watson: d = Σ(e_t - e_{t-1})²/Σe_t². d ≈ 2: no autocorrelation. d << 2: positive autocorrelation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Safety stock: z=1.28, σ_L=30. SS=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For D = 4900/yr, S = ₹50, H = ₹4/unit/yr, the minimum total ordering-plus-holding cost (at EOQ ≈ 350) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Resource leveling vs resource smoothing:
options: Leveling may extend project duration. Smoothing keeps critical path
questions.solution: Leveling: resolve over-allocation (may delay). Smoothing: adjust within float (no extension).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Capacity — demonstrated vs rated:
options: Demonstrated: historical output. Rated: theoretical max (designed capacity × utilization × efficiency)
questions.solution: Capacity: rated = D × U × E. Utilization = actual/max. Efficiency = actual/effective.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The simplex method is used to solve:
questions.solution: Simplex moves along the vertices of the feasible polytope to the optimum of an LP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Productivity is defined as the ratio of:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost ₹20000, price ₹120, variable cost ₹70/unit. The output needed for a target profit of ₹20000 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quality definition:
options: Fitness for use (Juran). Conformance to requirements (Crosby). Degree of excellence
questions.solution: Quality: meets/exceeds customer expectations. Dimensions: performance, features, reliability, etc.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lead time reduction strategies:
questions.solution: Postponement: finalize product at DC near customer. Reduces forecast risk and inventory.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FMEA (Failure Mode & Effects Analysis):
options: RPN = Severity × Occurrence × Detection. Prioritize high RPN items
questions.solution: FMEA: potential failure modes, effects, causes. RPN > 100 = high priority. Action: reduce S, O, or D.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Logistics network design:
options: Number/location of facilities. Trade-off: warehouse cost vs transportation cost. Total cost optimization
questions.solution: Network: gravity model, mixed-integer programming. 5-15% of GDP. Hub-and-spoke vs point-to-point.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Control chart — u chart:
options: Defects per unit (variable sample size). Average defects per unit
questions.solution: u-chart: u = c/n. UCL = ū + 3√(ū/n). LCL = ū - 3√(ū/n).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Contract types in procurement:
options: Fixed price, cost-plus, time & materials, incentive, unit price
questions.solution: Fixed price: risk on seller. Cost-plus: risk on buyer. FPI: shared risk/reward.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Acceptance sampling is used to decide whether to accept or reject a:
questions.solution: A sample is inspected and the lot accepted/rejected per the sampling plan.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Warehousing functions:
options: Receiving, put-away, storage, order picking, packing, shipping
questions.solution: Warehouse: unit load storage. Pallet racks, drive-in, push-back, AS/RS. Slotting optimization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A process has USL = 110, LSL = 90, mean = 100, σ = 3. Its Cpk is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Last-mile delivery economics:
options: Multi-stop route optimization. Time windows, traffic, failed delivery cost. Drone, locker, hub
questions.solution: Last mile: 28% of total logistics cost. Parcel lockers reduce failed delivery. Route density critical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Process capability ratio for non-normal:
options: Cpm, Cpmk. Taguchi loss function (off-target penalty)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MPS — time fence:
options: Demand time fence (DTF) and planning time fence (PTF). Within DTF: changes costly
questions.solution: Time fence: DTF (frozen). PTF (slushy). Beyond: free. Rolling horizon planning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two-handed process chart objective:
options: Balance work between left and right hands. Eliminate idle time
questions.solution: Two-handed: simo chart. Balance: both hands work simultaneously. Idle = wasted time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-normal control charts:
options: For skewed data: Ln transform, Box-Cox, distribution-based limits, median chart
questions.solution: Non-normal: Pareto, lognormal, Weibull. Use fitted distribution to set control limits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Single-period — overage cost Co:
options: Loss per unit over-ordered. c-s (cost - salvage value)
questions.solution: Newsvendor: if Cu > Co → SL > 0.5. Perishable goods, seasonal fashion, holiday items.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an LP, the function being optimized (maximized or minimized) is the:
questions.solution: The objective function expresses the goal (e.g. profit, cost) as a linear combination of decision variables.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A process has USL = 112, LSL = 88, mean = 102, σ = 2. Its Cpk is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 120, 110 and 120 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Project risk management — P-I matrix:
options: Probability × Impact → risk score (1-25). High: P5×I5=25. Risk response: avoid, transfer, mitigate, accept
questions.solution: Risk: P-I matrix. Expected monetary value (EMV) = P × I. Contingency reserve.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A task has a normal time of 6 min with 15% allowances. Its standard time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Acceptance sampling: producer's risk (α) is:
options: Probability of rejecting a good lot (Type I error)
questions.solution: α: P(reject | lot is good). Consumer's risk β: P(accept | lot is bad). OC curve shows both.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A task has a normal time of 6 min with 10% allowances. Its standard time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 3 and tp = 8 days. Its standard deviation is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 2 and tp = 11 days. Its standard deviation is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The PERT expected time of an activity (optimistic to, most-likely tm, pessimistic tp) is:
questions.solution: PERT uses a beta-distribution mean te = (to + 4tm + tp)/6.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Safety stock:
options: Buffer against demand variability and lead time variability
questions.solution: SS = z·σ_dLT (service level z, std dev of demand during lead time). Cycle service level.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For D = 6400/yr, S = ₹50, H = ₹2/unit/yr, the minimum total ordering-plus-holding cost (at EOQ ≈ 565.7) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 5000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹2/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI):
options: Supplier manages buyer's inventory. Reduced bullwhip effect
questions.solution: VMI: vendor monitors stock, triggers replenishment. CPFR: collaborative planning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 2000 units, ordering cost = ₹100/order, holding cost = ₹4/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 8000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹5/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about JIT is correct?
options: JIT reduces inventory by producing to actual demand
questions.solution: JIT pulls production to demand with small lots, cutting inventory and exposing waste.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Borg CR-10 scale measures:
questions.solution: Borg CR-10: category-ratio scale 0-10. Used for rating perceived exertion, pain, breathlessness. Subjective assessment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DOE — full factorial:
options: 2^k experiments. All factor combinations. Main effects + interactions
questions.solution: 2^k: k factors, 2 levels each. Effect = Σ(+)/n_+ - Σ(-)/n_-. Interaction AB: 1/2×(effect of A at B+ minus B-).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MRP (Material Requirements Planning) explodes the master schedule using the:
options: Bill of materials and inventory records
questions.solution: MRP nets requirements from the MPS, BOM and on-hand inventory to plan orders.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gage R&R — %GRR acceptance:
options: <10%: excellent. 10-30%: marginal (depends on risk). >30%: unacceptable. ndc > 4
questions.solution: %GRR = σ_GRR/σ_total. ndc = number of distinct categories = √2 × σ_part/σ_GRR. ndc ≥ 5.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Capital recovery factor converts:
options: Present value to equivalent annual cost
questions.solution: CRF = i(1+i)^n / ((1+i)^n - 1). Used for annualized cost of capital investments. Standard engineering economy tool.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) availability:
questions.solution: TPM availability: A = (run time - downtime) / planned production time. Component of OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Principles of motion economy:
options: Use of body, arrangement of workplace, design of tools/equipment
questions.solution: Motion economy: both hands work together, smooth continuous motions, gravity feed, drop delivery.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components of reliability 0.8 and 0.85 are in parallel. The system reliability is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If annual demand quadruples, the EOQ (other things equal) changes by a factor of:
questions.solution: EOQ ∝ √D, so a 4× demand gives √4 = 2× the order quantity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SPC — rational subgrouping:
options: Subgroup = homogeneous conditions. Instant of time vs consecutive units
questions.solution: Rational subgrouping: minimize within-group variation, maximize between-group variation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Game theory — pure vs mixed strategy:
options: Pure: always choose same action. Mixed: randomized choice (probability distribution)
questions.solution: Mixed: when no saddle point. Solve by linear programming or formula for 2×2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMAIC — Analyze phase tools:
options: Fishbone, FMEA, hypothesis testing, regression, ANOVA, multi-vari chart
questions.solution: Analyze: root cause identification. 5 Whys. Hypothesis test: t-test, F-test, chi-square.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Routing — operation sheet:
options: Detailed instructions for each operation: machine, tools, jigs, time
questions.solution: Operation sheet: sequence of operations. Route sheet: path through departments.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Markov chains:
options: State transitions with memoryless property. Steady-state probabilities
questions.solution: Markov: transition matrix P. Steady state: π·P = π. π_i = proportion of time in state i.
